pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's target Java version property from 17 to 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the build targets Java 17, failing to leverage Java 21 features and potentially causing compatibility issues with libraries requiring Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade jacoco-maven-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older JaCoCo versions may fail to instrument or run on Java 21 due to internal API changes or lack of support.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-checkstyle-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Checkstyle versions may fail to parse or analyze Java 21 syntax.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version that supports the 'release' flag and Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older compiler plugin versions may not support the 'release' flag or Java 21 compilation.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-war-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older WAR plugin versions may have compatibility issues with Java 21 toolchains.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated 'source' and 'target' compiler configuration with the modern 'release' flag.
pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' flags are deprecated in favor of 'release' for cross-compilation and consistency in modern Java versions.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add 'release' flag to the test compiler configuration to ensure tests are compiled for Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without the 'release' flag, tests might be compiled with incompatible settings or default to an older Java version.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct semantic annotation of serializable fields in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the new annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is not available without importing java.io.Serial, causing a compile error.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial, the compiler may warn or error depending on configuration, and it is required for correct serialization metadata in modern Java.

src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Import the java.io.Serial class to support the @Serial annotation.
src/main/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: The @Serial annotation is part of java.io in Java 21; without the import, the code will not compile.

src/test/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.general_idea: Add the @Serial annotation to the serialVersionUID field to comply with Java 21 serialization rules.
src/test/java/io/github/jhipster/sample/.why_exists: Java 21 introduces stricter checks for serialization; without @Serial on serialVersionUID, the code may fail to compile or trigger warnings/errors depending on the compiler configuration (e.g., --enable-preview or specific linting rules enabled by the migration recipe).
